title: Rising sea levels are making coastal flooding more common around the world; study finds
summary_full: Coastal flooding is becoming more frequent globally due to rising sea levels.  Extreme sea-level events now occur four times more often than a century ago.  Human-caused warming has amplified the impact of tides and storms.  Natural variations still influence local flood risk across coastlines.  Communities must rethink flood planning based on these changing probabilities.
